non_discriminating_assertions: A6: Retrieval vs grounding distinction (both passed)

diagnosis.reasoning: Mean benefit of +0.45 across all 3 evals with consistent improvement. The skill produces 100% pass rate on all assertions vs 55% baseline. The benefit is concentrated in three areas the baseline consistently misses: (1) Step 0 verification, (2) compound failure detection, (3) structured revert-first discipline.
highest_impact_areas: Step 0 — Verify actual context (discriminating in all 3 evals)
Compound failure detection (discriminating in all 3 evals)
Revert-first discipline for regressions (discriminating in eval 2)
non_discriminating_assertions: Basic category identification (buried context, missing context) — baseline already does this reasonably well
Quick fix suggestions — baseline produces decent ad-hoc fixes
wasted_context: Individual category descriptions for common categories (buried, missing) add tokens but baseline already identifies these. The skill's value is in the TRIAGE STRUCTURE and COMPOUND DETECTION, not in teaching what buried context is.
recommendations: The skill's unique value is Step 0, compound triage, and revert-first — consider making these even more prominent
Category descriptions for well-known issues (buried context, missing context) could be condensed since baseline agents already diagnose these
Consider adding more examples of compound failures since that's the highest-impact differentiator
